self._last_tick_config_hash: Optional[int] = None\n106\t\n107\t    async def _get_http_client(self) -> ClientSession:\n108\t        \"\"\"Get or create HTTP client.\"\"\"\n109\t        if \"\"\"Load monitoring configuration from YAML file (with hot-reload cache).\"\"\"\n115\t        logger.info(f\"Loading monitoring config from {self.config_path} (via hot-reload cache)\")\n116\t\n117\t        # Use config loader which handles mtime checking and caching\n118\t        data = await self._config_loader.get_config()\n119\t\n120\t        # Parse active topics\n121\t        active_topics = )\n163\t        return self.config\n164\t\n165\t    async def check_topic_state(self, rule: MonitoringRule) -> Optional[dict]:\n166\t        \"\"\"\n167\t        Check the current state of a topic.\n168\t\n169\t        Uses the fetch orchestrator with the full command matrix to get current state.\n170\t        \"\"\"\n171\t        # Map monitoring intent_type to fetch IntentType\n172\t        intent_type = INTENT_TYPE_MAPPING.get(rule.intent_type, IntentType.STATUS)\n173\t\n174\t        # Build fetch context\n175\t        context = FetchContext(\n176\t            project_slug=rule.project_slug,\n177\t            namespace=rule.project_slug.replace(\"-\", \"\") if rule.project_slug else None,\n178\t            repo_path=f\"/home/coding/{rule.project_slug}\" if rule.project_slug else None,\n179\t            app_name=rule.project_slug,\n180\t            deployment=rule.project_slug,\n181\t        )\n182\t\n183\t        # Create fetch request\n184\t        request = FetchRequest(\n185\t            intent_type=intent_type,\n186\t            context=context,\n187\t            intent_id=f\"monitoring-{rule.topic_id}\",\n188\t            session_id=\"monitoring\",\n189\t        )\n190\t\n191\t        # Execute fetch using the orchestrator (uses full fetch command matrix)\n192\t        try:\n193\t            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(request)\n194\t\n195\t            # Extract successful source data\n196\t            successful_data = fetch_result.get_successful_data()\n197\t\n198\t            if not successful_data:\n199\t                logger.warning(f\"No successful sources for {rule.topic_id}\")\n200\t                return None\n201\t\n202\t            # Build state data from all successful sources\n203\t            state_data = {\n204\t                \"project_slug\": rule.project_slug,\n205\t                \"intent_type\": rule.intent_type,\n206\t                \"sources\": list(successful_data.keys()),\n207\t                **{k: v for source in successful_data for k, v in successful_data[source].items()},\n208\t            }\n209\t\n210\t            # Apply filters if specified\n211\t            if rule.filters:\n212\t                if not self._passes_filters(state_data, rule.filters):\n213\t                    # Filter means we don't report this state\n214\t                    logger.debug(f\"State for {rule.topic_id} filtered by {rule.filters}\")\n215\t                    return None\n216\t\n217\t            logger.debug(f\"Fetched state for {rule.topic_id} from {len(successful_data)} sources\")\n218\t            return state_data\n219\t\n220\t        except Exception as e:\n221\t            logger.error(f\"Error checking state for {rule.topic_id}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n222\t            return None\n223\t\n224\t    def _passes_filters(self, state_data: dict, filters: list[str]) -> bool:\n225\t        \"\"\"\n226\t        Check if state data passes all filters.\n227\t\n228\t        Filters are simple expressions like \"phase!=Running\" or \"restarts>0\".\n229\t        Returns False if any filter fails.\n230\t        \"\"\"\n231\t        for filter_expr in filters:\n232\t            if not self._evaluate_filter(state_data, filter_expr):\n233\t                return False\n234\t        return True\n235\t\n236\t    def _evaluate_filter(self, state_data: dict, filter_expr: str) -> bool:\n237\t        \"\"\"\n238\t        Evaluate a single filter expression against state data.\n239\t\n240\t        Supports: !=, >, <, >=, <=, ==\n241\t        Examples: \"phase!=Running\", \"restarts>0\", \"ready!=1/1\"\n242\t        \"\"\"\n243\t        import re\n244\t\n245\t        # Parse filter expression\n246\t        match = re.match(r'(\\w+)(!=|>=|<=|>|<|==)(.+)', filter_expr)\n247\t        if not match:\n248\t            logger.warning(f\"Invalid filter expression: {filter_expr}\")\n249\t            return True\n250\t\n251\t        field_path, op, value = match.groups()\n252\t\n253\t        # Get field value from nested dict\n254\t        field_value = self._get_nested_value(state_data, field_path)\n255\t\n256\t        # Compare\n257\t        if field_value is None:\n258\t            return False\n259\t\n260\t        try:\n261\t            if op == \"!=\":\n262\t                return str(field_value) != value\n263\t            elif op == \"==\":\n264\t                return str(field_value) == value\n265\t            elif op == \">\":\n266\t                return float(field_value) > float(value)\n267\t            elif op == \"<\":\n268\t                return float(field_value) < float(value)\n269\t            elif op == \">=\":\n270\t                return float(field_value) >= float(value)\n271\t            elif op == \"<=\":\n272\t                return float(field_value) <= float(value)\n273\t        except (ValueError, TypeError):\n274\t            # If comparison fails, treat as no match\n275\t            logger.warning(f\"Filter comparison failed: {filter_expr} with value {field_value}\")\n276\t            return False\n277\t\n278\t        return True\n279\t\n280\t    def _get_nested_value(self, data: dict, path: str) -> Any:\n281\t        \"\"\"Get a value from nested dict using dot notation.\"\"\"\n282\t        keys = path.split(\".\")\n283\t        value = data\n284\t        for key in keys:\n285\t            if isinstance(value, dict):\n286\t                value = value.get(key)\n287\t            else:\n288\t                return None\n289\t        return value\n290\t\n291\t    async def _get_topic_context_cache(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n292\t        \"\"\"Get cached context data for a topic from topic_context_cache table.\"\"\"\n293\t        cached = await self._store.get_topic_context(topic_id)\n294\t        if cached:\n295\t            return cached.get(\"context\")\n296\t        return None\n297\t\n298\t    async def _update_topic_context_cache(\n299\t        self,\n300\t        topic_id: str,\n301\t        context_data: dict,\n302\t        ttl_seconds: int = TOPIC_CONTEXT_TTL_SECONDS,\n303\t    ) -> None:\n304\t        \"\"\"Store context data in topic_context_cache table.\"\"\"\n305\t        await self._store.set_topic_context(\n306\t            topic_id=topic_id,\n307\t            context_data=context_data,\n308\t            ttl_seconds=ttl_seconds,\n309\t        )\n310\t\n311\t    async def detect_state_change(\n312\t        self,\n313\t        rule: MonitoringRule,\n314\t        current_state: dict,\n315\t    ) -> tuple[bool, dict]:\n316\t        \"\"\"\n317\t        Detect if the state has changed since last check.\n318\t\n319\t        Reads from topic_context_cache to get previous state.\n320\t        Returns (has_change, changes_dict) where changes_dict tracks what changed.\n321\t\n322\t        The changes_dict includes:\n323\t        - 'changed_fields': list of field names that changed\n324\t        - 'diff': full diff between previous and current state\n325\t        - 'is_first': True if this is the first check (no previous state)\n326\t        \"\"\"\n327\t        # Get cached context from database\n328\t        cached_context = await self._get_topic_context_cache(rule.topic_id)\n329\t\n330\t        if cached_context is None:\n331\t            # First check - no previous state in cache yet\n332\t            # Store current state as baseline and don't notify\n333\t            await self._update_topic_context_cache(rule.topic_id, current_state)\n334\t            return False, {\n335\t                \"is_first\": True,\n336\t                \"changed_fields\": [],\n337\t                \"diff\": {},\n338\t            }\n339\t\n340\t        # Compute diff between previous and current state\n341\t        diff = self._compute_diff(cached_context, current_state)\n342\t        changed_fields = list(diff.keys())\n343\t\n344\t        # Check for state changes based on notification threshold\n345\t        if rule.notification_threshold == \"any_change\":\n346\t            # Any field change triggers notification\n347\t            has_change = len(changed_fields) > 0\n348\t        elif rule.notification_threshold == \"state_change\":\n349\t            # Only notify if specific state fields change\n350\t            # For status intents, check if phase, status, or health changed\n351\t            state_fields = [\"phase\", \"status\", \"health\", \"ready\", \"sync_status\", \"sync_status\"]\n352\t            significant_changes = [f for f in changed_fields if f in state_fields]\n353\t            has_change = len(significant_changes) > 0\n354\t        else:\n355\t            has_change = False\n356\t\n357\t        return has_change, {\n358\t            \"is_first\": False,\n359\t            \"changed_fields\": changed_fields,\n360\t            \"diff\": diff,\n361\t        }\n362\t\n363\t    async def push_monitoring_result(\n364\t        self,\n365\t        rule: MonitoringRule,\n366\t        current_state: dict,\n367\t        changes_dict: dict,\n368\t        session_id: str,\n369\t    ) -> None:\n370\t        \"\"\"\n371\t        Push a monitoring result to the active surface.\n372\t\n373\t        Creates a result in the session store with intent_id=NULL (system-originated).\n374\t        Reads previous state from topic_context_cache and updates it after writing result.\n375\t        \"\"\"\n376\t        # Find or \"notification_threshold\": rule.notification_threshold,\n396\t        }\n397\t\n398\t        # Derive result_type from monitoring intent type per plan §10\n399\t        result_type = session_id=session_id,\n406\t            summary=self._generate_summary(rule, current_state, previous_state, changes_dict),\n407\t            data=result_data,\n408\t )\n422\t\n423\t        # Update result's card_fallback flag\n424\t        await self._store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n425\t\n426\t        logger.info(f\"Created monitoring result {result_id} for topic {rule.topic_id} (intent_id=NULL)\")\n427\t\n428\t        # Fire SSE event for canvas update\n429\t        # Build result dict for         \"card_fallback\": render_outcome.card_fallback,\n440\t            \"rendered_html\": render_outcome.rendered_html,\n441\t            \"component_id\": render_outcome.component_id,\n442\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n443\t        }\n444\t\n445\t        # Broadcast to all surfaces in the session\n446\t        await broadcast_result(\n447\t            result=result_for_broadcast,\n448\t            session_id=session_id,\n449\t        )\n450\t\n451\t        logger.info(f\"Broadcast SSE event for monitoring result {result_id}\")\n452\t\n453\t        # Update topic context cache with current state\n454\t        await self._update_topic_context_cache(rule.topic_id, current_state)\n455\t\n456\t    def _compute_diff(self, previous: dict, current: dict) -> dict:\n457\t        \"\"\"Compute diff between previous and current state.\"\"\"\n458\t        diff = {}\n459\t\n460\t        for key in set(list(previous.keys()) + list(current.keys())):\n461\t            prev_val = previous.get(key)\n462\t            curr_val = current.get(key)\n463\t\n464\t            if prev_val != curr_val:\n465\t                diff[key] = {\n466\t                    \"from\": prev_val,\n467\t                    \"to\": curr_val,\n468\t                }\n469\t\n470\t        return diff\n471\t\n472\t    def _generate_summary(\n473\t        self,\n474\t        rule: MonitoringRule,\n475\t        current_state: dict,\n476\t        previous_state: Optional[dict],\n477\t        changes_dict: dict,\n478\t    ) -> str:\n479\t        \"\"\"Generate a summary for the monitoring result.\"\"\"\n480\t        if changes_dict.get(\"is_first\"):\n481\t            return f\"Initial state check for {rule.topic_id}\"\n482\t\n483\t        changed_fields = changes_dict.get(\"changed_fields\", [])\n484\t        diff = changes_dict.get(\"diff\", {})\n485\t\n486\t        # Check for specific state changes\n487\t        if \"phase\" in diff:\n488\t            prev_phase = diff[\"phase\"].get(\"from\")\n489\t            curr_phase = diff[\"phase\"].get(\"to\")\n490\t            return f\"{rule.topic_id} phase changed from {prev_phase} to {curr_phase}\"\n491\t\n492\t        if \"sync_status\" in diff:\n493\t            prev_sync = diff[\"sync_status\"].get(\"from\")\n494\t            curr_sync = {prev_health} to {curr_health}\"\n501\t\n502\t        if changed_fields:\n503\t            return f\"{rule.topic_id} changed: {', '.join(changed_fields[:3])}\" + (\"...\" if len(changed_fields) > 3 else \"\")\n504\t\n505\t        return f\"{rule.topic_id} state updated\"\n506\t\n507\t    async def monitor_topic(self, rule: MonitoringRule) -> None:\n508\t        \"\"\"\n509\t        Monitor a single topic on its check interval.\n510\t\n511\t        Runs in a loop until cancelled.\n512\t        \"\"\"\n513\t        logger.info(f\"Starting monitor for topic {rule.topic_id} (interval: {rule.check_interval}s)\")\n514\t\n515\t        # Use a default session for monitoring results\n516\t        # In full implementation, this would use the active session\n517\t        session_id = \"monitoring\"  # Placeholder\n518\t\n519\t        while self.running:\n520\t            try:\n521\t                # Check current state (uses fetch command matrix)\n522\t                current_state = await self.check_topic_state(rule)\n523\t\n524\t                if current_state:\n525\t                    # Detect change and get changes dict\n526\t                    has_change, {rule.topic_id}: {changes_dict.get('changed_fields', [])}\")\n530\t                        await self.push_monitoring_result(rule, current_state, changes_dict, session_id)\n531\t                    else:\n532\t                        # Update cache even if no change (keeps it fresh)\n533\t                        await self._update_topic_context_cache(rule.topic_id, current_state)\n534\t\n535\t                # Wait for next check\n536\t                await asyncio.sleep(rule.check_interval)\n537\t\n538\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n539\t                break\n540\t            except Exception as e:\n541\t                logger.error(f\"Error monitoring topic {rule.topic_id}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n542\t                await asyncio.sleep(rule.check_interval)\n543\t\n544\t    async def start(self) -> None:\n545\t        \"\"\"Start ambient monitoring.\"\"\"\n546\t        logger.info(\"Starting ambient monitoring service\")\n547\t\n548\t        # Load config\n549\t        config = await self.load_config()\n550\t\n551\t        self.running = True\n552\t\n553\t        # Start a monitor task for each active topic\n554\t        for rule in config.active_topics:\n555\t            task = asyncio.create_task(self.monitor_topic(rule))\n556\t            self.tasks.append(task)\n557\t\n558\t        # Start the ticker task for config hot-reload\n559\t        self._ticker_task = asyncio.create_task(self._config_ticker())\n560\t        self.tasks.append(self._ticker_task)\n561\t\n562\t        logger.info(f\"Started {len(self.tasks)} topic monitors (including config ticker)\")\n563\t\n564\t    async def _config_ticker(self) -> None:\n565\t        \"\"\"\n566\t        Config hot-reload ticker.\n567\t\n568\t        Periodically checks if the config file has changed (via mtime) and reloads if needed.\n569\t        Runs on the tick_interval_seconds from the config.\n570\t        \"\"\"\n571\t        logger.info(\"Config ticker started\")\n572\t\n573\t        while self.running:\n574\t            try:\n575\t                # Get current tick interval (may have changed from config)\n576\t                tick_interval = await self._config_loader.get_tick_interval_seconds()\n577\t                logger.debug(f\"Config ticker: checking for changes (interval: {tick_interval}s)\")\n578\t\n579\t                # Get current config (auto-reloads if mtime changed)\n580\t                current_config = await self._config_loader.get_config()\n581\t\n582\t                # Create a simple hash of the active topics to detect structural changes\n583\t                import json\n584\t                current_hash = hash(json.dumps(current_config.get(\"monitoring\", {}).get(\"active_topics\", []), sort_keys=True))\n585\t\n586\t                if self._last_tick_config_hash != current_hash:\n587\t                    logger.info(\"Config structure changed, reloading monitors\")\n588\t                    await self.reload_config()\n589\t                    self._last_tick_config_hash = current_hash\n590\t                else:\n591\t                    logger.debug(\"Config structure unchanged, no reload needed\")\n592\t\n593\t                # Wait for next tick\n594\t                await asyncio.sleep(tick_interval)\n595\t\n596\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n597\t                break\n598\t            except Exception as e:\n599\t                logger.error(f\"Error in config ticker: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n600\t                await asyncio.sleep(tick_interval)\n601\t\n602\t        logger.info(\"Config ticker stopped\")\n603\t\n604\t    async def stop(self) -> None:\n605\t        \"\"\"Stop ambient monitoring.\"\"\"\n606\t        logger.info(\"Stopping ambient monitoring service\")\n607\t        self.running = False\n608\t\n609\t        # Cancel all monitor tasks\n610\t        for task in self.tasks:\n611\t            task.cancel()\n612\t\n613\t        # Wait for tasks to complete\n614\t        await asyncio.gather(*self.tasks, return_exceptions=True)\n615\t\n616\t        self.tasks.clear()\n617\t        self._ticker_task = None\n618\t\n619\t        # Close HTTP client\n620\t        if self._http_client:\n621\t            await self._http_client.close()\n622\t\n623\t        logger.info(\"Ambient monitoring stopped\")\n624\t\n625\t    async def reload_config(self) -> None:\n626\t        \"\"\"Reload monitoring configuration and restart topic monitors.\"\"\"\n627\t        logger.info(\"Reloading monitoring configuration\")\n628\t\n629\t        # Separate ticker task from monitor tasks\n630\t        monitor_tasks = [t for t in self.tasks if t != self._ticker_task]\n631\t        self.tasks = [self._ticker_task] if self._ticker_task else []\n632\t\n633\t        # Cancel old monitor tasks (but not the ticker)\n634\t        for task in monitor_tasks:\n635\t            task.cancel()\n636\t        await asyncio.gather(*monitor_tasks, return_exceptions=True)\n637\t\n638\t        # Load new config and restart monitors\n639\t        config = await self.load_config()\n640\t\n641\t        for rule in config.active_topics:\n642\t            task = asyncio.create_task(self.monitor_topic(rule))\n643\t            self.tasks.append(task)\n644\t\n645\t        logger.info(f\"Reloaded monitoring config, started {len(self.tasks)} topic monitors\")\n646\t\n647\t\n648\t# Global ambient monitor def load_config(self) -> MonitoringConfig:\n        \"\"\"Load monitoring configuration from YAML file (with hot-reload cache).\"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Loading monitoring config from {self.config_path} (via hot-reload cache)\")\n\n        # Use config loader which handles mtime checking and caching\n        data = await self._config_loader.get_config()\n\n        # Parse active topics\n        active_topics = []\n        for item in data.get(\"monitoring\", {}).get(\"active_topics\", []):\n            active_topics.append(MonitoringRule(\n the full command matrix to get current state.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map monitoring intent_type to fetch IntentType\n        intent_type = INTENT_TYPE_MAPPING.get(rule.intent_type, IntentType.STATUS)\n\n        # Build fetch context\n        context = FetchContext(\n            project_slug=rule.project_slug,\n            namespace=rule.project_slug.replace(\"-\", \"\") if intent_id=f\"monitoring-{rule.topic_id}\",\n            session_id=\"monitoring\",\n        )\n\n        # Execute fetch using the orchestrator (uses full fetch command matrix)\n        try:\n self._passes_filters(state_data, rule.filters):\n                    # Filter means we don't report this state\n                    logger.debug(f\"State for {rule.topic_id} filtered by {rule.filters}\")\n                    return None\n\n            logger.debug(f\"Fetched state for {rule.topic_id} from {len(successful_data)} sources\")\n            return state_data\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Error checking state for {rule.topic_id}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n            return None\n\n    def _passes_filters(self, state_data: dict, filters: list[str]) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if state data passes all filters.\n\n        Filters are simple expressions like \"phase!=Running\" or \"restarts>0\".\n        Returns False if any filter fails.\n        \"\"\"\n        for filter_expr in filters:\n            if not self._evaluate_filter(state_data, filter_expr):\n                return False\n        return True\n\n    def _evaluate_filter(self, state_data: dict, filter_expr: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evaluate a single filter expression against state data.\n\n        Supports: !=, >, <, >=, <=, ==\n        Examples: \"phase!=Running\", \"restarts>0\", \"ready!=1/1\"\n        \"\"\"\n        import re\n\n        # Parse filter expression\n        match = re.match(r'(\\w+)(!=|>=|<=|>|<|==)(.+)', filter_expr)\n        if not match:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid filter expression: {filter_expr}\")\n            return True\n\n        field_path, op, value = match.groups()\n\n        # Get field value from nested dict\n        field_value = self._get_nested_value(state_data, field_path)\n\n        # Compare\n        if field_value is None:\n            return False\n\n        try:\n            if op == \"!=\":\n                return str(field_value) != value\n            elif op == \"==\":\n                return str(field_value) == value\n            elif op == \">\":\n                return float(field_value) > float(value)\n            elif op == \"<\":\n                return float(field_value) < float(value)\n            elif op == \">=\":\n                return float(field_value) >= float(value)\n            elif op == \"<=\":\n                return float(field_value) <= float(value)\n        except (ValueError, TypeError):\n            # If comparison fails, treat as no match\n            logger.warning(f\"Filter comparison failed: {filter_expr} with value {field_value}\")\n            return False\n\n        return True\n\n    def _get_nested_value(self, data: dict, path: str) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"Get a value from nested dict using dot notation.\"\"\"\n        keys = path.split(\".\")\n        value = data\n        for key in keys:\n            if isinstance(value, dict):\n                value = value.get(key)\n            else:\n                return None\n        return value\n\n    async def topic_context_cache to get previous state.\n        Returns (has_change, changes_dict) where changes_dict tracks what changed.\n\n        The changes_dict includes:\n        - 'changed_fields': list of field names that changed\n        - 'diff': full diff between previous and current state\n        - 'is_first': True if this is the first check (no previous state)\n        \"\"\"\n        # Get cached context from database\n        cached_context = await self._get_topic_context_cache(rule.topic_id)\n\n        if cached_context is None:\n            # First check - no previous state in cache yet\n            # Store current state as baseline and don't notify\n            await self._update_topic_context_cache(rule.topic_id, current_state)\n            return False, {\n                \"is_first\": True,\n                \"changed_fields\": [],\n                \"diff\": {},\n            }\n\n        # Compute diff between previous and current state\n        diff = self._compute_diff(cached_context, current_state)\n        changed_fields = list(diff.keys())\n\n        # Check for state changes based on notification threshold\n        if rule.notification_threshold == \"any_change\":\n            # Any field change triggers notification\n            has_change = len(changed_fields) > 0\n        elif rule.notification_threshold == \"state_change\":\n            # Only notify if specific state fields change\n            # For status intents, check if phase, status, or health changed\n            state_fields = [\"phase\", \"status\", \"health\", \"ready\", \"sync_status\", \"sync_status\"]\n            significant_changes = [f for f in changed_fields if f in state_fields]\n            has_change = len(significant_changes) > 0\n        else:\n            has_change = False\n\n        return has_change, {\n            \"is_first\": False,\n            \"changed_fields\": changed_fields,\n            \"diff\": diff,\n        }\n\n    async def push_monitoring_result(\n        self,\n        rule: MonitoringRule,\n        current_state: dict,\n        changes_dict: dict,\n        session_id: state.\"\"\"\n        diff = {}\n\n        for key in set(list(previous.keys()) + list(current.keys())):\n            prev_val = previous.get(key)\n            curr_val = current.get(key)\n\n            if prev_val != curr_val:\n                diff[key] = {\n                    \"from\": prev_val,\n                    \"to\": curr_val,\n                }\n\n        return diff\n\n    def _generate_summary(\n        self,\n        rule: MonitoringRule,\n        current_state: dict,\n        previous_state: Optional[dict],\n        changes_dict: dict,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate a summary for the monitoring result.\"\"\"\n        if changes_dict.get(\"is_first\"):\n            return f\"Initial state check for {rule.topic_id}\"\n\n        changed_fields = changes_dict.get(\"changed_fields\", [])\n        diff = changes_dict.get(\"diff\", {})\n\n        # Check for specific state changes\n        if \"phase\" in diff:\n            prev_phase = diff[\"phase\"].get(\"from\")\n            curr_phase = diff[\"phase\"].get(\"to\")\n            return f\"{rule.topic_id} phase changed from {prev_phase} to {curr_phase}\"\n\n        if (via mtime) and reloads if needed.\n        Runs on the tick_interval_seconds from the config.\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(\"Config ticker started\")\n\n        while self.running:\n            try:\n                # Get current tick interval (may have changed from config)\n                tick_interval = await self._config_loader.get_tick_interval_seconds()\n                logger.debug(f\"Config ticker: checking for changes (interval: {tick_interval}s)\")\n\n                # Get current config (auto-reloads if mtime changed)\n                current_config = await self._config_loader.get_config()\n\n                # Create a simple hash of the active topics to detect structural changes\n                import json\n                current_hash = hash(json.dumps(current_config.get(\"monitoring\", {}).get(\"active_topics\", []), sort_keys=True))\n\n                if self._last_tick_config_hash != current_hash:\n                    logger.info(\"Config structure changed, reloading monitors\")\n                    await self.reload_config()\n                    self._last_tick_config_hash = current_hash\n                else:\n                    logger.debug(\"Config structure unchanged, no reload needed\")\n\n                # Wait for next tick\n                await asyncio.sleep(tick_interval)\n\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                break\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error in config ticker: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                await asyncio.sleep(tick_interval)\n\n        logger.info(\"Config ticker stopped\")\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop ambient monitoring.\"\"\"\n        logger.info(\"Stopping ambient monitoring service\")\n        self.running = False\n\n        # Cancel all monitor tasks\n        for task in self.tasks:\n            task.cancel()\n\n        # Wait for tasks to complete\n        await asyncio.gather(*self.tasks, return_exceptions=True)\n\n        self.tasks.clear()\n        self._ticker_task = None\n\n        # Close HTTP client\n        if self._http_client:\n            await self._http_client.close()\n\n        logger.info(\"Ambient monitoring stopped\")\n\n    async def reload_config(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Reload monitoring configuration and restart topic monitors.\"\"\"\n        logger.info(\"Reloading monitoring configuration\")\n\n        # Separate ticker task from monitor tasks\n        monitor_tasks = [t for t in self.tasks if t != NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_session ON feedback_signals(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_type ON feedback_signals(signal_type);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_processed ON feedback_signals(processed);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_result ON feedback_signals(result_id);\n\n-- Per-stage latency capture for every dispatch (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n-- Keyed by the intent *thread* id (routed_intent.intent_id — the same id the\n-- fetch/synthesize/escalate strands and the results row use), NOT the\n-- intents.id the caller mints in create_intent(). router_ms is shared across\n-- every intent thread from the same utterance; escalate_ms is NULL for hot-path\n-- dispatches; stt_ms/first_render_ms are client-reported and NULL when the\n-- client never reports them. synthesize_first_token_ms is NULL until the\n-- synthesize strand streams (the non-streaming call_simple path can't measure\n-- first token separately) — see src/instrument/timings.py.\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dispatch_timings (\n    intent_id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    router_ms                 INTEGER,\n    fetch_first_source_ms     INTEGER,\n    fetch_total_ms            INTEGER,\n    synthesize_first_token_ms INTEGER,\n    synthesize_total_ms       INTEGER,\n    escalate_ms               INTEGER,\n    sse_emit_ms               INTEGER,\n    stt_ms                    INTEGER,\n    first_render_ms           INTEGER,\n    created_at                INTEGER NOT NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dispatch_timings_created ON dispatch_timings(created_at);\n\n-- Bead watch table: circuit breaker tracking for async beads\n-- Persistence layer for the async-path circuit breaker (plan §10 The Async Path).\n-- Tracks open beads watched by the BeadWatcher, recording refusal counts,\n-- last refusal reasons, SLA deadlines, and fencing state. All state is persisted\n-- so a watcher restart loses nothing -- breaker state lives in the database,\n-- not only in memory.\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bead_watch (\n    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,  -- References bead ID (intents.bead_ref)\n    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of REFUSED: comments seen\n    last_refusal_reason TEXT,  -- Most recent refusal reason\n    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,  -- Timestamp of most recent refusal\n    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT -1,  -- Latest comment index processed (-1 = none)\n    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- Unix timestamp when SLA expires\n    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when SLA was flagged (NULL if not flagged)\n    fenced_at          INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when bead was fenced to status=blocked (NULL if not fenced)\n    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL  -- When this watch row was created\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch(sla_deadline);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch(fenced_at);\n\n-- Card cache: pre-rendered HTML for result components\n-- Stores server-side rendered HTML for result/component/layout combinations.\n-- Primary key (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket) allows multiple cached\n-- variations per result (e.g., different layouts). Populated after component\n-- selection to avoid repeated rendering for the same result.\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS card_cache (\n    result_id      TEXT NOT NULL,\n    component_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n    layout_bucket  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    rendered_html  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    created_at     INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    PRIMARY KEY (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_card_cache_result_id ON card_cache(result_id);\n\"\"\"\n\n# The set of dispatch_timings timing columns record_dispatch_timings() may set.\n# intent_id/created_at are handled explicitly (PK / insert-only timestamp).\nDISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS = (\n    \"router_ms\",\n    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n    \"escalate_ms\",\n    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n    \"stt_ms\",\n    \"first_render_ms\",\n)\n\n# Valid intent status values (plan §10 The Async Path: stuck/failed added for circuit breaker)\nINTENT_STATUSES = ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')\n\n# Default SLA hours per intent type (plan §10 The Async Path: Visible Aging)\n# task-profile: 6h (async bead-backed tasks)\n# hot-path intents: 30s (budget is 3s, flag at 10x for aged pending)\nDEFAULT_SLA_HOURS: dict[str, float] = {\n    \"task-profile\": 6.0,  # 6 hours for async bead-backed tasks\n    \"status\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path status intents\n    \"action\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path action intents\n    \"lookup\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path lookup intents\n    \"brainstorm\": 0.5,   # 30 minutes for brainstorm (may need user iteration)\n    \"reminder\": 24.0,    # 24 hours for reminders\n}\n\n# Circuit breaker thresholds (plan §10 The Async Path)\nCIRCUIT_BREAKER_REFUSAL_THRESHOLD = 3  # Fence after N refusals\nCIRCUIT_BREAKER_AGE_THRESHOLD_HOURS = 24.0  # Fence after N hours without progress\n\n\nclass SessionStore:\n    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for concurrent access.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, db_path: Path):\n        self.db_path = db_path\n\n    async def initialize(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Initialize database with schema and WAL mode.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n\n            # Create schema\n            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n            await db.commit()\n\n            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:","numLines":200,"startLine":100,"totalLines":1937}}}
